title: War on Iran: Spiralling Out of Control Under Trump
content:
The ongoing conflict involving Israel, Iran, and their neighbors has spiraled out of control in the Middle East. Some observers argue that if international arrest warrants for alleged war crimes had detained Benjamin Netanyahu earlier, immense suffering might have been avoided. Instead, the Israeli prime minister’s lifelong obsession with eradicating perceived threats has fueled a seemingly endless cycle of violence. This 'forever war' mentality has increasingly drawn the United States into the fray under former President Donald Trump. While past U.S. leaders sought to hold Israel in check, Trump has often given Netanyahu free rein to mount unprecedented joint military operations.

This aggressive approach has led to fears of a dramatic escalation, including potential strikes on energy infrastructure or the deployment of ground troops. Critics argue that Trump's strategy treats the conflict almost like a video game, relying on simplistic narratives and flattening complex geopolitical realities. Despite occasionally signaling a desire to de-escalate, Trump has deferred to Netanyahu's hardline policies at crucial moments. The fundamental question now is whether anyone will actually listen if and when Trump decides to try to end the war. Without a clear diplomatic strategy, the region remains trapped in a volatile and devastating holding pattern.
